theme: sculpt a shape out of avalanches
title: Avalanche Sculptor
seed_problem: abelian sandpile on an NxN grid; drop grains and stabilize by toppling
mutation: composite: turn sandpile stabilization into an inverse-design objective -- reconstruct a published target stable configuration by choosing grain counts at a fixed set of drop sites, under a grain budget, with internal sink walls (boundary-shaping) that split avalanches. Because toppling is abelian the joint pile is a linear superposition of the per-site drops, so the objective couples abelian dynamics + boundary shaping + superposition-linearity.
innovation_hook: Arbitrary targets are reachable by a linear combination of drops across many sources, not by tuning one pile.
strategies: trivial: drop nothing -> empty grid matches exactly the zero cells of the target (checker baseline)
greedy (trap): tune a SINGLE pile -- pick the one source+amount that scores best; reproduces one localized fractal lobe and misses the rest
strong (insight): exploit abelian superposition -- coordinate ascent that combines drops across MANY sources, re-stabilizing incrementally, to reconstruct the multi-lobe target
beyond strong: solve the integer toppling equation T = D - L*u for u>=0 with D supported on the sources (reduced-Laplacian / least-action view)
